generalCitation: Limitations Act, 2002, S.O. 2002, c. 24, Sched. B, s. 4

credit-card.notes: Ontario's 2-year basic limitation period applies from the date the debt was discovered or the last date of activity (payment or written acknowledgement). Many credit card agreements have an acknowledgement clause — ensure no payments have been made recently.
credit-card.citation: Limitations Act, 2002, S.O. 2002, c. 24, Sched. B, s. 4

medical.notes: Medical debts in Ontario are subject to the standard 2-year limitation period. The clock typically starts from the date the bill was due and unpaid.
medical.citation: Limitations Act, 2002, S.O. 2002, c. 24, Sched. B, s. 4

auto-loan.notes: Auto loan deficiency balances (remaining amount after vehicle repossession and sale) are subject to the 2-year limitation period, typically running from the date of the deficiency notice.
auto-loan.citation: Limitations Act, 2002, S.O. 2002, c. 24, Sched. B, s. 4

personal-loan.notes: Personal loans are subject to Ontario's standard 2-year limitation period from the date the debt was acknowledged or last payment made.
personal-loan.citation: Limitations Act, 2002, S.O. 2002, c. 24, Sched. B, s. 4

student-loan.notes: Private student loans follow Ontario's 2-year limitation period. Government-guaranteed OSAP loans may have different rules — consult a lawyer or the National Student Loans Service Centre for federal loan timelines.
student-loan.citation: Limitations Act, 2002, S.O. 2002, c. 24, Sched. B, s. 4

mortgage-deficiency.notes: Mortgage deficiency claims in Ontario may be subject to a 10-year limitation period under the Real Property Limitations Act, but many are governed by the 2-year basic period depending on the type of action taken. Consult a lawyer for your specific situation.
mortgage-deficiency.citation: Real Property Limitations Act, R.S.O. 1990, c. L.15, s. 4; Limitations Act, 2002, s. 4
mortgage-deficiency.lawName: Real Property Limitations Act / Limitations Act, 2002

utility-bill.notes: Unpaid utility bills are subject to Ontario's standard 2-year limitation period from when the amount was due and unpaid.
utility-bill.citation: Limitations Act, 2002, S.O. 2002, c. 24, Sched. B, s. 4

regulatoryBody: Ministry of Public and Business Service Delivery — Consumer Protection Ontario
complaints: File a complaint at ontario.ca or call 1-800-889-9768. Collection agencies in Ontario are licensed under the Collection and Debt Settlement Services Act, R.S.O. 1990, c. C.14.
